assertions.text: The vehicle left the roadway (truck departed the road).
The truck struck a traffic signal pole (impact with pole).
There was a single impact in the crash (only one distinct collision occurred).
After the impact with the traffic signal pole, the truck came to rest (truck stopped after the collision).
No other impacts occurred and the crash consisted only of a single impact with the pole (implying the collision sequence was limited to that one impact).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows speed samples decreasing from [157.8 km/h](T2) at about [t=-5.0 s](T1) to [122.4 km/h](T2) at [t=0.0 s](T2), and a detected speed drop event from [157.8 km/h to 133.6 km/h](T3), but there is no GPS lateral position, road-edge, or lane-departure channel in the available data to confirm the vehicle left the roadway; therefore the statement is unverifiable from sensors alone.

The crash-pulse shows cumulative longitudinal delta-V peaking at [7.3 km/h](T2) (final reported [7.7 km/h](T3)) and lateral delta-V peaking at [4.3 km/h](T2,T3), indicating a real impact, but there is no channel that identifies the struck object (no object-type or external collision direction mapping). The device-frame axes are undocumented so impact being specifically with a traffic signal pole cannot be determined from telemetry alone; thus unverifiable.

The EDR/impact detections report a single crash-pulse event with the cumulative longitudinal delta-V reaching [7.7 km/h at t=0.251 s](T3) and lateral delta-V reaching [4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T3), and the EDR summary lists [num_events_recorded: 3] but only one crash-pulse/delta-V impact is present in the pulse channels (delta_v_long/lat) as shown by their single final values [7.3 km/h final_value](T2); this supports the assertion of a single distinct impact recorded by the vehicle.

Speed samples show the vehicle speed decreased from [157.8 km/h at t=-5.0 s](T2) to [122.4 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2) and the crash-pulse cumulative delta-V reaches a final longitudinal change of [7.3 km/h](T2) during impact, and the brake channel shows a transition to ON at [t=-0.5 s](T2,T3); following the impact there are no additional delta-V pulses recorded (final delta_v_long [7.3 km/h final_value](T2)), which supports that the truck came to rest after the collision relative to the recorded crash pulse (i.e., no subsequent impacts detected).

The crash-pulse channels show a single cumulative longitudinal delta-V peaking at [7.7 km/h](T3) and lateral at [4.3 km/h](T3) with no additional impact pulses detected in the delta_v channels (delta_v_long final_value [7.3 km/h](T2)), and detect_events returned one impact_delta_v per axis (T3); this supports that the crash sequence consisted only of that single recorded impact and no other impacts were recorded by the vehicle sensors.

result.rationale: The telemetry clearly records one crash pulse (delta-V) and no subsequent pulses, supporting a single-impact sequence and a stop after impact, but it contains no position or object-identification channels to verify roadway departure or that the struck object was a traffic signal pole; therefore the narrative is partly supported and partly unverifiable.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
